title: World model maker Odyssey nabs $1.45B valuation backed by Amazon and other big names
content_snippet: World models are the next big thing in AI beyond LLMs and, with this round, Odyssey has cemented itself as one of the startups to watch.
title_zh: 世界模型制造商Odyssey获1.45亿美元估值，亚马逊等巨头参投
summary_zh: 世界模型初创公司Odyssey在新一轮融资中获得1.45亿美元估值，投资方包括亚马逊等知名机构。此举巩固了其在LLM之后AI领域的下一个风口——世界模型赛道的领先地位。
key_points: Odyssey完成新一轮融资，估值达1.45亿美元
投资方包括亚马逊等知名大型企业
世界模型被视为继大语言模型后的AI新前沿

title: New survey: ~half of Americans don't recognize Sam Altman or Dario Amodei. Does name recognition shape how AI gets judged?
content_snippet: A national survey compared favorability and name recognition for 8 major tech executives, and the recognition gap is what stood out. The people most associated with building AI, Altman, Amodei, Huang, are unknown to a third to a half of the country, while opinions about tech as a whole keep getting measured through Musk and Zuckerberg, who most people know and view negatively. Tim Cook was the only one clearly above water. If most Americans can't name the people
title_zh: 调查显示约半数美国人不认识Sam Altman或Dario Amodei
summary_zh: 一项全国性调查对比了8位科技高管的知名度和好感度，发现Altman、Amodei等AI领军人物在美国有三分之一到一半的人不认识，凸显了AI行业领袖的公众认知度差距。
key_points: 约半数美国人不认识Sam Altman或Dario Amodei
调查对比了8位科技高管的公众知名度与好感度
AI领军人物在公众认知度上存在显著差距

title: Two-thirds of Americans think AI is advancing too quickly
content_snippet: According to the latest Pew Research poll, 49 percent of Americans report using chatbots at least occasionally, but 63 percent think the tech is advancing too quickly. Overall, use of AI chatbots has increased dramatically since 2024, when only 33 percent reported using them. Specifically, ChatGPT's usage has doubled since 2023, with 44 percent of […]
title_zh: 皮尤研究：三分之二美国人认为AI发展过快
summary_zh: 皮尤研究中心最新民调显示，49%的美国人偶尔使用聊天机器人，但63%认为AI技术发展过快。自2024年以来，AI聊天机器人的使用率从33%大幅上升。
key_points: 63%的美国人认为AI技术发展过快
AI聊天机器人使用率从2024年的33%升至49%
公众对AI发展速度的担忧与使用率同步增长

title: Only 16 percent of Americans think AI will have a positive impact on society, a new study shows
content_snippet: Although Wall Street loves AI, every day Americans are significantly less optimistic about the industry, a new report from Pew Research shows.
title_zh: 研究显示仅16%美国人认为AI将对社会产生积极影响
summary_zh: 皮尤研究中心报告指出，尽管华尔街看好AI，但普通美国人对该行业持悲观态度，仅有16%的人认为AI将对社会产生积极影响，反映出公众信心严重不足。
key_points: 仅16%的美国人认为AI对社会有积极影响
公众对AI的乐观情绪远低于华尔街
皮尤研究揭示了公众对AI的深层担忧

title:
I made Physical AutoResearch sound simple (conceptually), but it took a village to pull off and lots of design thinking into the robot /loopcraft. The hardest part is everything we need to setup *before* pressing Enter. Here's a behind-the-scene tour:

1. Safety harness

Letting 8 robots run unattended overnight means safety has to be more than a hint in the system prompt. ENPIRE hardwires it in 2 layers: (1) hard kinematic limit that trips an immediate task failure and auto-resets as soon as a robot leaves its safety envelope, and (2) a torque-limited compliant gripper so a bad contact or misaligned insertion ends in a safe stall, instead of crushing the robot or the object at hand. 

We make safety more conservative than usual so humans can sleep tight. In reality, we still need a few human operators to watch over the "robots of loving grace". 

2. Definition of /done

An agent that can edit its own reward will game it for sure. ENPIRE fixes the goalposts before the fleet can move them. Here's the recipe:

Collect a few minutes of success & failure demos
-> Ask agent to write code using computer vision tools to classify success and measure against groundtruth
-> Agent hill-climbs on classifier until reliably good
-> This classifier becomes the real-time reward function that directly computes on sensor streams 
-> *Freeze* the reward function before AutoResearch. It's sacred, enshrined in a Gym env that no one can touch.

3. System telemetry design

Robot-seconds is by far the scarcest resource, followed by GPU-seconds, and finally tokens. We instrument all three and surface them to ENPIRE for live resource awareness rather than letting it hill-climb in a vacuum. 

We define:
- Mean Robot Utilization ("MRU"): the fraction of wall-clock time when the robot is actively executing an experiment. Otherwise the hardware is sitting idle and waiting for the next code commit.
- Mean Token Utilization ("MTU"): tokens consumed per minute, our proxy for how hard the agent is actually thinking. A low MTU means the agent is stalled, waiting on a robot rollout to finish instead of doing research.
- GPU utilization: fraction of wall-clock time when GPU is active. 

... and evaluate on two budget-to-outcome metrics:

1. Tokens-to-Success: token budget the fleet burns to complete /goal.
2. Time-to-Success: wall-clock time to /goal
content_snippet:

1. Safety harness

title_zh: Jim Fan详解物理自动研究系统：安全、奖励与遥测设计
summary_zh: 英伟达科学家Jim Fan分享了构建物理自动研究机器人系统的幕后技术细节，包括双层安全机制、固定奖励函数防止作弊，以及多维度资源利用率监控等关键设计。
key_points: 设计双层硬安全机制确保机器人无人值守运行
在实验前固化奖励函数，防止AI代理作弊
定义多种资源利用率指标用于优化系统效率

title: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
content_snippet: Google is betting generative AI can breathe new life into the smart speaker. The company's new $99.99 Google Home Speaker replaces the rigid commands of the Google Assistant era with more conversational Gemini interactions.
title_zh: 谷歌押注Gemini重塑智能音箱
summary_zh: 谷歌希望通过生成式AI为其智能音箱注入新活力。新款99.99美元的Google Home Speaker放弃了Google Assistant的刚性指令，转而采用更自然的Gemini对话式交互。
key_points: 谷歌发布新款99.99美元的Gemini智能音箱
新音箱采用对话式交互，取代传统刚性指令
谷歌希望通过生成式AI重振智能音箱品类

title: Vibe-decoding the White House-Anthropic fight over Fable
content_snippet: Hello and welcome to Regulator, an email for Verge subscribers about technology, politics, and what happens when science crashes headlong into self-interest. Not a subscriber? Sign up here today! Got the scoop on a petty feud that's going to somehow fundamentally reshape the entire field of frontier AI development? Send 'em over to tina.nguyen+tips@theverge.com. Back […]
title_zh: 解读白宫与Anthropic围绕Fable的纷争
summary_zh: 文章分析了特朗普政府与AI公司Anthropic之间因Fable而起的公开争执，将其视为一场可能重塑前沿AI领域的政治与科技利益冲突。
key_points: 白宫与Anthropic就Fable事件发生公开争执
此次纷争可能影响前沿AI领域的未来格局
事件反映了政治与科技利益的直接碰撞

title: I made AI Boost so I could stop repeating myself constantly
content_snippet:
I'm guessing a lot of people use LLMs in a similar way to me: basically maintaining a billion projects in parallel.

Because of this, I tend to re-use patterns over and over that come from my experience as a web developer in the before time. I say things like "look at X project in Y folder to see how it's done there.".

I got a bit tired of this, so I made AI Boost (https://ai-boost.io) (Yes, I use Claude,
title_zh: 开发者制作AI Boost工具以解决重复提示问题
summary_zh: 一位开发者分享了自己制作的AI Boost工具，旨在帮助同时管理多个项目、频繁重复使用固定模式指令的用户，减少与LLM交互时的重复工作。
key_points: 开发者创建AI Boost工具以减少重复性提示
工具旨在帮助并行管理多个项目的用户
解决与LLM高频交互中的模式复用痛点

title: Ten months later, the $100 Google Home Speaker is finally available for preorder
content_snippet: Google's new smart speaker is more about Gemini than audio quality.
title_zh: 售价100美元的谷歌智能音箱在十个月后开放预订
summary_zh: 谷歌新款Gemini驱动的智能音箱在发布十个月后终于开放预购，售价100美元，将于6月25日发售。该产品更侧重于AI功能而非音质。
key_points: 谷歌Gemini智能音箱售价100美元，开放预购
产品将于6月25日正式发售
智能音箱主打AI功能，音质为次要考量
